rephrased_requirement: Develop a secure, high-performance AI feature that lets authenticated users upload files, extracts the content, and enables them to ask natural-language questions about the uploaded material, returning accurate answers while protecting data privacy.
functional_requirements: Provide a web UI and API endpoint for users to upload files.
Validate uploaded file types and enforce size limits.
Scan uploaded files for malware before processing.
Extract text/content from supported file formats (PDF, DOCX, TXT, images via OCR).
Store extracted content in a searchable vector store.
Expose an endpoint for users to submit natural-language queries referencing their uploaded files.
Generate answers using a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline that combines the vector store with a large language model.
Return the answer with source citations and confidence scores.
Allow users to delete their uploaded files and associated data.
non_functional_requirements: Response latency for a query must be ≤ 2 seconds for 95 % of requests.
File upload latency must be ≤ 5 seconds for files up to 10 MB.
System must handle at least 200 concurrent users with graceful degradation.
Scalable architecture to support growth in file volume and query load.
security_requirements: All data in transit must be encrypted with TLS 1.3.
Data at rest must be encrypted using AES-256.
User authentication via OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect with JWT tokens.
Authorization checks to ensure users can only access their own files and queries.
Automated malware scanning (e.g., ClamAV) on every uploaded file.
Isolation of processing environments (container sandbox) to prevent code execution from malicious files.
Audit logging of upload, query, and delete actions.
Compliance with GDPR / CCPA – ability to purge user data on request.
Annual penetration testing and adherence to ISO 27001 controls.
acceptance_criteria: A user can successfully upload a supported file and receive a confirmation within the defined latency.
Malware-infected files are rejected and logged.
After upload, the user can ask at least three distinct questions and receive answers with source citations within 2 seconds.
Answer accuracy on a curated test set of 100 questions is ≥ 90 % (precision) and hallucination rate ≤ 5 %.
All data transfers are encrypted; storage encryption can be verified via bucket policies.
Only the file owner can retrieve or delete the file; attempts by other users are denied with a 403 response.
System meets the defined performance benchmarks under a simulated load of 200 concurrent users.
Audit logs contain timestamped entries for every upload, query, and delete operation.
recommended_solution_approach: Implement a cloud-native RAG service. Use object storage (e.g., AWS S3) with presigned URLs for secure uploads, trigger a Lambda/Cloud Function to run virus scanning and content extraction, then index the text in a managed vector database (e.g., Pinecone or OpenSearch k-NN). Deploy a containerized LLM inference service (or use a managed LLM API) behind an API Gateway with OAuth2/JWT authentication. Cache recent query results in Redis to meet latency targets. Employ Terraform for IaC, CI/CD pipelines for automated testing, and monitoring via Prometheus/Grafana with alerts on latency and security events.
open_questions: What is the maximum allowed file size and which additional file formats should be supported?
Should the solution use a third-party LLM (e.g., OpenAI) or a self-hosted model, and what are the cost constraints?
What is the expected retention period for uploaded files and extracted embeddings?
Are there any specific regulatory compliance requirements beyond GDPR/CCPA (e.g., HIPAA)?
What authentication provider will be used (e.g., Azure AD, Okta)?
Is there a preferred cloud provider or must the solution be multi-cloud/on-premise?
What is the budget for compute resources, especially for vector storage and LLM inference?
How will answer quality be measured and who will maintain the benchmark dataset?
